title: Wormhole Guardian Signature Verification Failure
summary: An attacker forged a valid-looking guardian approval path and minted wrapped ETH on Solana without locking ETH on Ethereum, leading to roughly $326M in losses before Jump backstopped the hole.

classification.privilegeGained: ability to mint wrapped assets without a valid cross-chain message
classification.exploitMechanism: signature verification bypass in Solana bridge contract path

response.summary: The bridge was paused, patched, and recapitalized by Jump so the wrapped asset remained redeemable.

attackPath.title: Craft malicious Solana message
attackPath.description: The attacker prepared a cross-chain message payload that exploited a verification path expected to enforce guardian authenticity.

attackPath.title: Bypass guardian signature checks
attackPath.description: A verification bug allowed the attacker to satisfy the bridge's acceptance logic without a legitimately approved guardian set message.

attackPath.title: Mint unbacked wrapped ETH on Solana
attackPath.description: The bridge accepted the forged message and minted wrapped ETH that was not backed by locked ETH on Ethereum.

attackPath.title: Bridge and extract value
attackPath.description: The attacker moved part of the newly minted assets through markets and cross-chain paths to extract value before the bridge was halted.

evidence.claimSupported: The exploit created unbacked wrapped ETH on Solana, extraction followed, and Jump later recapitalized the missing collateral.
Wormhole's security model depends on guardian-signed messages and Solana-side verification of that cross-chain attestation path.
The attack led to roughly $326M in losses and became one of the largest bridge incidents.

similarIncidentHints: Multichain trust and cross-chain control incidents
